text:
Assistant: **Epic created: [nostr-multi-platform#2858](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2858)** — no epic existed before (the surveyor confirmed nothing ties this workstream together; the closest ancestors, NMP #2626/#2627, are closed). I cross-linked it from chirp#30 and NMP#2809, and yes: **time travel is in the plan as committed scope (Phase E), not a stretch goal** — per your message, it's written up as the recorded/replayable debugging capsule with scrubbing, not log viewing. [...] ## Epic/tracking issue check
**No epic currently ties this workstream together.** Nothing tracks "NMP X-Ray diagnostic surface / nmp-devtools + Trellis trace tooling + Chirp diagnostics pane" as one initiative. #2809, #114 (closed), and #30 cross-reference each other explicitly but aren't rolled up under a tracking issue. Nearest analogues: trellis #87 (showcase/Observatory epic — adjacent but scoped to demo apps) and NMP's now-closed #2626/#2627 (the reconciliation-substrate epic that preceded this). [...] - #2809 ADR-0075 amendment: diagnostic surface is not app surface — propose nmp-devtools — the core proposal for the X-Ray/devtools diagnostic surface; open, `status:needs-adr` [...] - **Phase A — ADR-0075 amendment** (#2809): define "diagnostic surface" vs "app surface," and update doctrine-lint plus the Trellis public-API leak ratchet (#2642) so the exemption is enforced mechanically, not case-by-case.
- **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`. [...] ## ADR-0075 current stance
`docs/decisions/0075-trellis-private-reconciliation-substrate.md` in nostr-multi-platform. Decision: NMP may use Trellis as *private* reconciliation machinery below typed read sessions ("Trellis owns generic mechanics. NMP owns Nostr and product meaning."). Draws one boundary: Trellis primitives must never become app/native/web-facing or appear in builder docs; NMP owns resource identity semantics even where Trellis owns identity mechanics. "Boundaries" forbids "exported Trellis types in app/native/web APIs" with no carve-out for a diagnostic/tooling surface; "Enforcement" says doctrine tests/public API checks reject raw Trellis primitives in "app/native/web-facing NMP surfaces" — silent on any dev/diagnostic surface. Does not currently mention `nmp-devtools`, receipts, or diagnostics at all. This is exactly the gap #2809 proposes to close by adding a second, dev-build-only "diagnostic surface" category. Related links: ADR-0070 (typed read sessions), ADR-0076 (app-facing feed helpers), epics #2626/#2627 (both closed). [...] **NMP#2809** — OPEN. Labels: priority:p2, category:decision, status:needs-adr, area:architecture. Proposes amending ADR-0075 to split "app surface" (unchanged: no Trellis vocabulary crosses UniFFI/web) from a new "diagnostic surface": a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate exposing Trellis trace/audit data via an MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus redacted trace export for bug reports. Explicitly excludes metrics/dashboards/log aggregation — receipts only. Names four upstream trellis prerequisites: #114 (serializable traces), #125 (audit retention toggle), #122 (typed resource keys), #126 (scope reclamation) — **all four now closed**. Sequenced after NMP #2808 (shadow→authority promotion), which is also now closed/merged (PR #2832, "Promote Trellis feed-session reconciliation to authority," merged today, 2026-07-03). Every stated prerequisite is done; #2809 is unblocked, just needs the ADR decision. No comments. [...] **chirp#30** — OPEN. Labels: enhancement. Proposes an "X-Ray Feed" developer overlay showing live relay-subscription reconciliation with causal chains, staged in three phases: v0 "Shadow X-Ray" (buildable now, visualizes NMP's discarded shadow ResourcePlan next to legacy behavior — now stale since #2808 already promoted shadow to authority), v1 "Authority X-Ray" (real applied commands with causal chains, gated on #2808 — now closed/done), v2 "Time travel" (record + scrub a session trace, gated on trellis#114 — now closed/done — and NMP#2809). Explicitly out of scope: metrics/dashboards/log views, receipts only. No comments. [...] **5. [Should-fix — Phase A] "Compiled out of release builds" needs a mechanical definition and CI enforcement.** `cfg(debug_assertions)` is the wrong tool (release-with-debug builds exist; per-platform build configs differ). Suggested wording: nmp-devtools is a separate crate behind an off-by-default cargo feature; CI asserts each platform's release artifact dependency graph (`cargo tree`) and/or symbol table contains no nmp-devtools; the ratchet rule is dependency-graph-based — an allowlist file of crates permitted to depend on `trellis-*` (existing private adapters + nmp-devtools), where adding a line is the ratchet violation.
